summary: Blazing Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence and soaring Scarlet Begonias
blurb: The second set delivers the legendary Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower marathon, backed by a ripping Not Fade Away into Saint Stephen. Jerry’s lead work is fire‑brand, while Keith adds sparkle on They Love Each Other and Scarlet Begonias. The rhythm section drives hard, making this ’76 Oakland show a standout in the catalog.
review: On October 9 1976 at Oakland-Alameda County Stadium the Dead turned in one of their most electrifying performances of the era. After a solid first set that featured a tight New Minglewood Blues and a bright Scarlet Begonias, the band launched into a second‑set that still reverberates through the archives: a thunderous Not Fade Away segues straight into Saint Stephen, which then erupts into the now‑mythic Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower sequence. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is on fire throughout – from the soaring leads on Scarlet Begonias to an epic solo that lifts Franklin’s Tower into another dimension – and his tone benefits from a freshly‑minted Fender Twin Reverb. Keith Godchaux shines on the intricate piano work in They Love Each Other and adds shimmering texture to the jam‑heavy moments. Phil Lesh pounds out authoritative bass lines, especially on the opening of Help and the driving Not Fade Away groove, while the twin drummers Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann keep a solid, propulsive pulse; the Samson & Delilah drum feature is noted as a hot highlight. The show’s energy never wanes, closing with a high‑octane One More Saturday Night and an exuberant U.S. Blues encore. Across all available tapes – from crisp soundboard masters to superb matrix mixes – the performance holds up as a quintessential 1976 Dead experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All but one of the eight recordings are described with overwhelmingly positive language – 'blistering', 'fire‑brand', 'legendary' – and multiple sources highlight the same standout moments (Help‑Slip‑Franklin, Scarlet Begonias, Not Fade Away into Saint Stephen). No consistent negative comments appear, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp, balanced mix; captures the full show without dropouts and only minor phase quirks that are easily corrected.

key_highlights: Help On The Way → Slipknot! → Franklin's Tower sequence is fire‑brand and a show‑defining centerpiece.
Scarlet Begonias shines with soaring Garcia leads and Keith’s sparkling keyboards.
Not Fade Away into Saint Stephen launches an explosive second set that never loses momentum.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers fire‑brand leads throughout, highlighted by an epic solo on Franklin's Tower and soaring phrasing on Scarlet Begonias; his tone benefits from a fresh Fender Twin Reverb.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides pounding, authoritative bass lines – especially on the opening of Help and the driving Not Fade Away groove – and adds solid support on Sugaree and New Minglewood Blues.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is solid and driving; his contributions to the Samson & Delilah drum feature add heat to the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in with a tight, propulsive pulse; together with Hart they create a powerful rhythmic foundation throughout the show.
